disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A large involuntary leak of urine happens the instant she coughs, laughs or lifts — provoked by the strain, with no warning.
trigger_question_examples: Tell me exactly what happens when you leak.
Does the leaking happen with coughing or laughing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It happens the second I cough — a big splash comes out with the pressure. Same if I laugh hard or lift something. It's the cough setting it off every time.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: There is no sudden, compelling, desperate need to pass urine before the leak — the leak is not preceded by urgency.
trigger_question_examples: Before you leak, do you get a sudden desperate urge to go?
Is there any warning that you're about to leak?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there's no warning urge at all. I don't feel a desperate need to go — it just leaks out when I cough.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A viral cold with a violent, paroxysmal cough over the last 48 hours is the acute trigger that turned occasional leaks into constant heavy leaking.
trigger_question_examples: What changed in the last couple of days?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had a rotten cold and a violent cough for two days. That's when the leaking went from a little bit to happening every single time I cough.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Constant wetness has left the skin of the perineum and inner thighs red, raw and burning.
trigger_question_examples: How is the skin in that area?
You mentioned burning — can you describe it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The skin between my legs and on my inner thighs is red raw and stinging from being wet all the time. It burns when fresh urine hits it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Background of years of small leaks with laughing/lifting, three prior vaginal deliveries of large babies, and being postmenopausal.
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me a bit about your background — pregnancies, periods?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've leaked a little with laughing or lifting for years. I had three big babies, all natural deliveries, and I've been through menopause.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No burning or stinging when actually passing urine, no blood in the urine, and no increase in how often she passes urine.
trigger_question_examples: Does it burn or sting when you actually wee?
Have you noticed any blood in your urine, or going more often?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it doesn't burn when I actually go, there's no blood, and I'm not going any more often than normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: She can still pass urine normally and empty her bladder; there is no inability to void and no painful lower-abdominal swelling or bloating.
trigger_question_examples: When you go to the toilet, can you pass urine normally and empty out?
Any painful swelling or fullness low in your tummy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, when I go I can pass urine fine and empty out normally. My tummy isn't swollen or painful and bloated — it's the leaking with the cough that's the problem.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Over-the-counter cough medicine has been taken with little effect.
trigger_question_examples: Have you tried anything for the cough?
Is the cough medicine helping at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been taking an over-the-counter cough medicine but it's barely touched the cough.
